page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This configuration consists of 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. The processor includes a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 260 MB L3 cache, along with advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals a significant contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in single-core metrics, including Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core performance is weak, as evidenced by Geekbench multi-core and PassMark CPU scores. Memory performance is a notable constraint, with uncached read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks falling into the poor category.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-highcpu-2 offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints or local storage. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution, but the low RAM-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and poor memory bandwidth limit its utility for memory-intensive databases. It is best suited for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, and basic text processing workloads that can leverage its strong single-core capabilities and advanced instruction sets.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 1 physical core with 2 threads and a 260 MB L3 cache. The processor supports advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core performance, particularly in single-threaded compression and Geekbench single-core tests. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are weak to poor. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for dedicated, compute-focused workloads with low memory requirements. It is best suited for single-threaded processing, lightweight text processing, and basic web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, delivering strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, strong single-core performance, and advanced vector and matrix instruction sets.

Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Weak multi-core performance and poor uncached memory bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for single-threaded compute tasks and light text processing
